content.description:
A declarative way to define how an item is displayed without writing JavaScript code. View-specs use `ui_hints` to describe which fields to show and how.

## When to Use View-Spec vs View

| Use View-Spec when... | Use [View](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) when... |
|-----------------------|---------------------------|
| Standard form-like layout | Custom visual design |
| Displaying/editing fields | Complex interactivity |
| Rapid prototyping | Conditional rendering |
| No custom logic needed | Computed displays |

## Structure

```javascript
{
  id: crypto.randomUUID(),
  name: "my-type-view-spec",
  type: "bbbbbbbb-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",  // view-spec
  content: {
    for_type: "my-type-uuid",
    displayName: "My Type Editor",
    description: "Standard editing view for my-type items",
    ui_hints: [
      { field: "title", label: "Title", component: "text", mode: "editable" },
      { field: "description", label: "Content", component: "markdown", mode: "editable" },
      { field: "created", label: "Created", component: "timestamp", mode: "readonly" }
    ]
  }
}
```

## The `ui_hints` Array

Each hint describes one field to render:

| Property | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `field` | Path to the value in `item.content` (e.g., `"title"`, `"metadata.author"`) |
| `label` | Human-readable label shown above the field |
| `component` | Which [field-view](item://cccccccc-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) to use |
| `mode` | `"readonly"` or `"editable"` |

## Available Components

| Component | Purpose | Modes |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| `text` | Single-line text | readonly, editable |
| `textarea` | Multi-line plain text | readonly, editable |
| `markdown` | Markdown with rendering | readonly, editable |
| `code` | Syntax-highlighted code | readonly, editable |
| `checkbox` | Boolean toggle | readonly, editable |
| `number` | Numeric input | readonly, editable |
| `timestamp` | Date/time display | readonly |
| `json` | Raw JSON editing | readonly, editable |
| `heading` | Section header (display only) | readonly |

## How It Works

[system:generic-view](item://b3e6eac6-631d-4131-b446-bab697f428be) interprets view-specs:

1. Loads the view-spec for the item's type
2. Iterates through `ui_hints`
3. For each hint, loads the appropriate field-view component
4. Renders field-views with values from `item.content`
5. In editable mode, field changes are saved back to the item

## Example: Note Type View-Spec

```javascript
ui_hints: [
  { 
    field: "title", 
    label: "Title", 
    component: "heading",
    mode: "editable" 
  },
  { 
    field: "description", 
    label: "Content", 
    component: "markdown", 
    mode: "editable" 
  },
  { 
    field: "tags", 
    label: "Tags", 
    component: "tags", 
    mode: "editable" 
  }
]
```

## Extending with Custom Components

Create new [field-view](item://cccccccc-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) items to add custom components. Once created, reference them by name in `ui_hints`.

## See Also

- [kernel:view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Imperative view code
- [field-view](item://cccccccc-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Field-level rendering components
- [system:generic-view](item://b3e6eac6-631d-4131-b446-bab697f428be) — The view that interprets view-specs
